app.name: Peeky
settings.title: Peeky Settings
settings.mode.label: Active mode
settings.mode.hint: What Peeky does when it speaks (also: Ctrl+Shift+M).
settings.history.empty: No history yet — Peeky's past remarks will show up here.
error.config_incomplete: Set your API Base URL, key, and model name in settings first (Ctrl+Shift+S).
error.capture: Couldn't capture the screen — grant Screen Recording permission in System Settings.
error.empty_response: The model returned an empty reply — try raising max tokens (some models force a hidden "thinking" mode that eats the budget).
error.screen_permission: Screen Recording permission is required to capture — enable Peeky in System Settings › Privacy & Security › Screen Recording, then restart the app.
settings.screenperm.label: Screen Recording
settings.screenperm.hint: Peeky needs this to see your screen. After granting it you usually have to restart the app.
settings.screenperm.black: Granted, but capture is black — quit & reopen Peeky
error.screen_black: Capture is black — Screen Recording permission usually needs a full quit & restart of Peeky to take effect (especially flaky in dev mode; test with the built app).
ask.hint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter newline · Esc to cancel
capture.hint: Drag to select a region · Esc to cancel
settings.baseUrl.label: Base URL
settings.baseUrl.hint: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (cloud, self-hosted, or local).
settings.apiKey.label: API Key
settings.apiKey.hint: Stored locally. May also be provided via the PEEKY_API_KEY environment variable.
settings.model.label: Model name
settings.maxTokens.label: Max output tokens
settings.maxTokens.hint: Upper bound on each reply length.
settings.temperature.label: Temperature
settings.temperature.hint: Higher = more varied, lower = more focused.
settings.reasoning.label: Reasoning effort
settings.reasoning.hint: How much the model 'thinks' before replying: lower = faster, higher = more thorough. Most models (e.g. Step 3.7 Flash) reason by default; lowering it speeds replies noticeably. Note: 'Off' depends on the provider — StepFun only supports low/medium/high, so Off may be ignored or error there (use Low).
settings.sensitivity.label: Trigger sensitivity
settings.sensitivity.hint: How easily a screen change wakes Peeky up.
settings.budget.label: Speech budget (per hour)
settings.budget.hint: Max number of proactive remarks per hour. Extra moments are saved silently.
settings.quietHours.label: Quiet hours
settings.quietHours.hint: During this window Peeky stays quiet. May wrap past midnight.
settings.followDnd.label: Follow system Do Not Disturb
settings.followDnd.hint: When macOS Focus / DND is on, Peeky stays quiet automatically.
settings.screenshotQuality.label: Screenshot quality
settings.screenshotQuality.hint: Higher quality improves recognition but uses more tokens.
settings.permission.label: Permission mode
settings.permission.hint: Controls how much Peeky may act on its own (only affects doing, not seeing or speaking).
settings.language.label: Language
settings.language.hint: UI and reply language. Auto follows your system locale.
settings.mascot.heading: Make it your own mascot
settings.mascot.intro: Want a Peeky of your very own? Copy the snippet below, change the description, and send it to the Codex installed on your computer.
settings.mascot.template:
Please generate a custom mascot for "Peeky", a desktop screen companion. Theme: <describe your mascot here, e.g. "an orange cat wearing a wizard hat">.
Requirements:
1. Generate an animated sprite set (sprite sheet / frame sequence) covering these states:
   idle / scanning / thinking / talking / has-something / working / paused
   A few frames per state, consistent style, smooth between frames, transparent background.
2. Replace the generated assets directly into the app's mascot resource directory:
   <APP_ASSETS_DIR>/mascot/<state>/frame_*.png
   (one folder per state, incrementing file numbers, overwrite existing files).
3. When done, quit and restart the "Peeky" app so the new mascot takes effect.
settings.showStats.label: Show token usage stats
settings.showStats.hint: Display call and token counters in this panel.
settings.stats.hidden: Token stats are hidden. Enable "Show token usage stats" to see them.
settings.pause.label: Observation
mode.roast.desc: A witty friend by your side.
mode.nerd.desc: Adds background you might not know.
permission.yolo.desc: Acts autonomously; still bound by hard limits.
